steps.title: Locate the clause boundary
steps.content: The key signal is that "humans were likely inhabiting the area" (after "In 1420 CE, according to Burger,") is an independent clause with its own subject and verb, requiring a proper sentence boundary from the main clause about the team's evidence.

steps.title: Apply the sentence separation rule
steps.content: A period correctly separates two independent clauses into two sentences. Choice A produces: "...evidence that it was occupied earlier. In 1420 CE, according to Burger, humans were likely inhabiting the area." with proper punctuation at the boundary. The other three options place a comma instead, creating a comma splice that improperly joins two independent clauses. That matches choice A — A is correct.
